Sydney / Melbourne | Contract | Hybrid

We are seeking an experienced Business Analyst to join a dynamic team supporting business and technology initiatives across enterprise environments.

This is an exciting opportunity for a highly analytical professional who enjoys gathering requirements, solving business challenges, and working closely with stakeholders to deliver meaningful outcomes.

You will play a key role in bridging the gap between business needs and technology solutions while contributing to impactful projects and transformation initiatives.

Proven experience as a Business Analyst

Strong experience in requirements gathering and documentation

Experience working across technology and business projects

Strong problem-solving and analytical skills

Excellent communication and stakeholder management abilities

Hybrid work flexibility

Contract opportunity with enterprise project exposure

Exposure to business and technology transformation projects

In partnership with our Federal Government Agency we are looking to engage a Lead Data Engineer, a strategic thinker ready to drive cloud-scale data solutions that power regulatory assurance and national initiatives.

The Opportunity:

This isn't a "business as usual" engineering role. We are looking for a leader who understands that data integrity is the backbone of trust. You will be responsible for:


Building and validating enterprise data solutions using Azure Synapse and Data Lake architectures.
Leading critical data migration and integration for high-impact change programs.
Embedding testability and quality by design, to ensure long-term maintainability and accurate reporting.
Driving the adoption of new practices and fostering growth within the engineering team.

You're more than a technical expert; you can translate complex pipelines into actionable insights for any stakeholder, fostering collaboration across multiple delivery teams.

Deep expertise in the Azure stack (Synapse, Data Lake, CI/CD).
High proficiency in SQL, Python, and PySpark.
A proven track record with Medallion architectures and scalable ELT/ETL frameworks.
A future-focused mindset, staying ahead of emerging trends in AI and data engineering.

Job Title: Senior Commercial Analyst (Supply Chain & Retail) - 12-month role
Ad Text:
The Opportunity 

Troocoo are partnered with a multinational services organisation to recruit a skilled and commercially astute Senior Commercial Analyst for a 12-month fixed-term contract.

This high-impact role focuses on driving data-informed procurement decisions, automating reporting, and supporting tender and negotiation activities across supply chain and retail categories.

The successful candidate will work with complex datasets, rebate structures, and stakeholders in a fast-paced environment where analysis directly influences costs and profitability.

Key responsibilities include

Provide analytical support for supply tenders and negotiations, including pricing templates and baselines.
Structure and consolidate complex data for analysis, including product attributes and unit conversions.
Develop scenario modelling to evaluate procurement options and supplier combinations.
Build and enhance Power BI dashboards for spend reporting, data integrity, price reviews, and product mapping.
Analyse complex rebate structures to ensure decisions reflect true net costs.
Contribute to process improvement, mapping (Visio), documentation, and automation.
Translate complex data into clear, actionable insights for non-financial stakeholders.

Skills and Experience

Strong commercial finance or analysis experience with large, complex datasets.
Advanced Excel (Power Query and formulas) and proficient Power BI skills.
Demonstrated procurement analysis experience, ideally in hospitality, food service, or FMCG.
Solid understanding of rebate structures and their impact on true cost (essential).
Strong financial acumen including P&L and GL knowledge.
Excellent communication skills and ability to simplify complex information.
Process-oriented with experience in mapping and automation.

Apply

Click ‘Apply’ or contact Jack Green, Senior Consultant – Accounting, Finance and Risk on jac•••••••@troocoo.com / 04•• ••• 645 for further information

Are you an immediately avaiable Analyst who's looking to use your expertise to make a difference? Let's talk!

I'm currently partnering with a leading healthcare business in recruiting for their next commercial business analyst.

The role will primary focus of this role will be managing pricing & discount calculations and processes, creating financial modelling simulation, project profitability review and preparation of business cases. In addition you will coordinate with the commercial team to provide contracts, tender & pricing support. You will be a valued partner to the commercial team while making a positive impact to the community.

This will suit candidate who are immediately avaialble or on short-notice. Healthcare sector experience is also an advantage. 


ABOUT THE ROLE

Reporting to the Head of Sales, you will be looking after the following but not limited to:

Prepare recommendations, secure management approval, and oversee follow-up activities through to execution.
Build, update, and refine financial models to support pricing, investment decisions, and project evaluations.  
Collaborate with internal stakeholders to gather inputs and assumptions, prepare projections, and generate commercial offers.
Perform scenario and sensitivity analyses to support data-driven business decisions.
Calculate CFP, list prices, discounts, and other pricing or cost-related metrics.
Act as the key liaison on pricing and cost matters with commercial and finance stakeholders.
Review project profitability across direct sales arrangements and projects.
Prepare comprehensive business cases for projects requiring Investment Committee approval, including financial analysis, assumptions, and risk assessment
Create and present materials for management review and approval at country and regional levels.
Take end-to-end ownership of projects from initiation through to execution.
Define project scope, timelines, and deliverables, ensuring milestones are met.
Prepare variance analyses and recommend corrective actions where required.
Create tools to monitor contracted accounts’ adherence to commercial terms to ensure compliance and profitability.
Support tender submissions and pricing strategies through market research, competitor analysis, and financial evaluation.

ABOUT YOU

Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Finance, Economics, or a related discipline
CPA, CA, CFA, or equivalent professional certification strongly preferred.
5–7 years’ experience in a commercial business analyst, commercial finance, corporate finance, or similar role within a complex or matrixed organisation.
Proven experience in commercial business analysis, financial modelling, business case development, and contract management.
Demonstrated capability in evaluating business development opportunities and supporting investment or governance decision-making.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and critical-thinking skills with a high level of attention to detail.
Advanced financial modelling skills, including scenario and sensitivity analysis.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to influence and collaborate effectively with senior and cross-functional stakeholders.
Ability to manage multiple priorities independently and deliver outcomes in a fast-paced environment.
Knowledge of relevant industry regulations, governance, and compliance requirements.
ABOUT THE BENEFITS

6-8 months contract, working closely with sales and finance in a global multinational environment, with potential to go permanent for high performers
$65-75 per hour + super
Very close to public transport, street parking available
Be a trusted partner to sales department with tender process
Part of a high performing team
For immediate start

Take hold of a monster opportunity and join our Data and AI team as a Data Quality Specialist.  If you’re a good fit for this role and live in Brisbane, Sydney or Melbourne, we’d love you to apply. 

Why join us?

You'll get 14% super as standard, to help your future come alive.
Unleash your potential and build a career to be proud of through excellent training and development opportunities.
Balance your work between in-person days at our purpose-built hubs and working from home.
In the office, enjoy end of trip facilities, a yoga and multi-faith room, parent and carer rooms, quiet rooms, BrewHub coffee facilities, and collaborative team spaces.

About the role

We’re all about helping our members make the most of their money. And while they go after their goals, you can too. As a Data Quality Specialist, you will play a critical enterprise‑wide role ensuring our data is accurate, reliable and fit for purpose across key data domains. In this role, you will:

Lead the development and implementation of enterprise‑wide data quality frameworks, standards and practices.
Define, monitor and report on data quality metrics, KPIs and dashboards across critical datasets.
Partner closely with data owners, stewards and custodians to embed data quality controls into business processes.
Oversee data profiling, cleansing and remediation activities to address data quality issues at their source.
Drive continuous improvement by undertaking root cause analysis and implementing pragmatic corrective actions.
Support the selection, implementation and optimisation of data quality tools and platforms.
Provide strategic input into broader data governance and data management initiatives.
Ensure data quality practices align with regulatory obligations and internal risk and compliance frameworks.

This role is a permanent opportunity. 

📍 Location: Brisbane, Sydney or Melbourne. 

About you

It goes without saying you'll be a great communicator with top notch interpersonal skills. We'll also expect you to pick up problems and come up with quick, creative ways to solve them. It's quite likely you tick some of the following boxes too:  

Proven enterprise‑level data quality expertise within a regulated industry, ideally financial services or superannuation.
Strong hands‑on experience in data quality management, data governance or data operations.
A deep understanding of data quality dimensions, root cause analysis and remediation techniques.
Experience working with data quality and governance tools such as Informatica, Talend, Ataccama, Collibra or similar platforms.
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to explain data quality concepts clearly to technical and non‑technical stakeholders.
A collaborative approach, with demonstrated ability to influence, engage and partner across diverse business areas.
A pragmatic mindset, balancing governance requirements with practical, business‑aligned solutions.
Knowledge of regulatory and compliance requirements impacting data, including APRA, ASIC and privacy obligations.

About us

Our 2.4 million members trust us to take care of over $330 billion in retirement savings. And we help them take charge of their finances and face the future with confidence. Our inclusive culture means you’ll be valued and heard in a respectful workplace. We've created an environment where you can be yourself and do amazing work. It's that simple.

Apply online now

Everyone is welcome to apply. We value diverse thinking, cultures, perspectives, backgrounds, and abilities. Awaken your career at Australian Retirement Trust and apply now. Applications close 11:30pm 28 May 2026. 

We don’t want to leave you with a monstrous wait. So, you’ll hear from us after the closing date to find out whether you’ve made it through to the next round or not. Being fair and up-front is important to us – and recruitment is no different.

Schneider Electric is the global leader in energy procurement and compliance services, with over 35 years’ experience helping clients reduce energy spend and improve efficiency. Managing more than $30 billion in annual energy spend across 300,000+ sites worldwide, our SE Advisory Services division continues to grow, driven by a passion for delivering smarter, more sustainable energy solutions.

SE Advisory Services provides independent consultancy services to large energy users, including renewable energy procurement strategies, energy efficiency identification, sustainability data management and reporting.

Our Melbourne CBD-based team is seeking a Senior Energy Commodity Market Modelling Analyst to join the Global Research & Analytics (GRA) team, supporting both local and global clients.

About the Role

This is a senior, client-facing role where you will act as a subject matter expert in Australian electricity and gas markets. You will lead modelling initiatives, deliver market insights, and support strategic decision-making through advanced data analytics and forecasting.

Key Responsibilities

Lead the development, maintenance, and enhancement of electricity and gas market models
Deliver forecast insights and present findings to internal stakeholders and external clients
Analyse large, complex datasets to identify market trends, risks, and opportunities
Monitor and interpret political, economic, and regulatory developments impacting energy markets
Produce high-quality market outlooks and research reports
Engage with clients to explain modelling outputs and support consulting engagements
Perform ad-hoc market analysis including statistical, fundamental, and technical assessments
Collaborate with global teams to develop market views and innovative modelling approaches

 

About You

Strong interest in energy markets, policy, and supply/demand dynamics
Experience in energy market analysis, modelling, or related quantitative field
Proven experience developing and managing forecasting models and working with large datasets
Degree in Economics, Mathematics, Statistics, Finance, or a related discipline (postgraduate qualifications advantageous)
Strong programming capabilities (e.g., Python, Matlab or similar)
Advanced Excel and data analysis skills
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to translate complex insights into clear, concise messages
Client-focused mindset with strong problem-solving capabilities
Ability to manage multiple priorities and deliver outcomes in a fast-paced environment

Highly Regarded

Experience in Australian electricity or gas market forecasting
Experience in big data analytics and visualisation
Exposure to global energy markets

The Perks

We offer a competitive salary package, including access to our employee share plan and salary continuance insurance. In addition, you’ll benefit from:

Flexible working arrangements
Strong commitment to pay equity and inclusive practices
Family-friendly leave policies
Global career development opportunities through our Career Hub
Access to global projects, internal mobility, and mentoring programs

If you’re looking to take the next step in your career with a leading global organisation and work on impactful energy market challenges, we’d love to hear from you.

Applicants must have full working rights in Australia at the time of application and will be required to provide evidence if shortlisted.

Schneider Electric does not accept unsolicited resumes from agencies. Any unsolicited profiles will be treated as direct applications.

Work with a leading commercial employer for the Canberra region and help steer them through their next period of growth and change.  This complex, multi-faceted organisation has been successfully operating in the Canberra region for over 100 years and are one of the ACT’s largest employers.

They are currently investing heavily in the next evolution of its asset management, lifecycle planning, and predictive analytics capability.

This role sits within the specialist Asset Lifecycle team of 8 people made up of Asset Strategy Engineers and Principal Engineers. Led by a manager is new to the organisation, however brings extensive asset management experience who is a supportive leader who takes a consultative approach to their staff and has already made a positive impression internally within the organisation
 
The Opportunity

This is a newly created role focused on uplifting the organisation’s asset intelligence and predictive asset management capability across a large-scale critical infrastructure environment.
 
You’ll combine engineering, analytics, and lifecycle planning to help shape how the organisation uses asset data to drive long-term investment, maintenance, and network risk decisions.

Own and shape the asset intelligence capability within the Asset Lifecycle team
Apply deterioration, replacement forecasting, and scenario-based lifecycle models
Help mature predictive maintenance and condition-based asset management approaches
Long-term focus on digital twin capability, analytics uplift, and strategic forecasting
The Role
Develop and maintain asset data models, pipelines, and analytics integrating condition, operational, and performance data to support lifecycle planning and forecasting
Build and refine long-term asset replacement (Repex) and deterioration models to support investment planning, regulatory submissions, and strategic network risk management
Deliver dashboards, reporting, and analytical insights enabling engineers and planners to prioritise maintenance and intervention activities
Improve asset data governance, taxonomy, and data quality across engineering and operational datasets
Work closely with engineering, field, planning, and digital teams to translate operational asset data into practical and actionable lifecycle insights
The Ideal Person

You likely come from an infrastructure-heavy environment where asset management, reliability, lifecycle planning, and engineering analytics are core to operational decision-making.

Essential experience

Experience within utilities, infrastructure, heavy industry, transport, or similar asset-intensive environments
Strong understanding of asset lifecycle management, reliability engineering, deterioration modelling, or condition-based maintenance principles
Experience working with asset-related datasets, engineering systems, and analytical tooling such as SQL, Python, Power BI, GIS, SCADA, EAM or similar platforms
Collaborative and consultative working style with the ability to engage engineers, operational teams, and senior stakeholders and produce practical business insights and recommendations
Naturally proactive with a continuous improvement mindset and strong ownership mentality around capability uplift and long-term outcomes
Salary / Rate
$148k - $174k + 16% super
Canberra based role, must be locally based or willing to relocate (relocation support available)
Permanent role with industry leading employer

At the NSW Department of Education, we educate and inspire lifelong learners – from early childhood, through schooling to vocational education and training.  




Temporary Full-Time until 31/12/2026

Located in Parramatta with flexible work arrangements available  

Salary: Clerk Grade 11/12 with salary ranging from $149,739 to $173,174 + super and annual leave loading

Shape the future of finance reporting through innovation, analytics and insight 


 

About the role

 

An exciting opportunity exists for a Business Intelligence Specialist to join the Finance team within the NSW Department of Education. 




In this role, you will lead the delivery of Finance’s business intelligence strategy, providing data-driven insights, reporting solutions and visualisations that support strategic decision making across the Department. 




Working closely with Finance leaders and stakeholders, you will help strengthen reporting capability, improve data governance and ensure high-quality financial information and reporting outcomes. 




For further information please click here to view the role description 




About you  


You are a strategic and solutions-focused professional with strong experience in business intelligence, financial reporting and data analytics within a large and complex environment. 




To be successful in this role, you will demonstrate: 




Extensive experience in business intelligence, financial reporting and data visualisation  

Strong analytical and problem-solving capability with high attention to detail  

Experience working with large and complex data sets while ensuring data quality and integrity  

Knowledge of data governance, reporting frameworks and business intelligence best practice  

Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills, including the ability to influence and provide advice to non-technical audiences  







Key knowledge and experience  
 

Extensive experience in the design, delivery and maintenance of business intelligence strategies and governance protocols.  

Knowledge of and commitment to implementing the Department’s Aboriginal Education Policy and upholding the Department’s Partnership Agreement with the NSW AECG and to ensure quality outcomes for Aboriginal people. 




Essential requirements of the role 

Qualifications in a relevant discipline or demonstrated equivalent knowledge, skills and experience.  

Demonstrated understanding of and commitment to the value of public education.  







Benefits 

Flexible working arrangements

35 hour working week

Competitive salary, salary sacrificing options, employer’s contribution to superannuation and annual leave loading

Flex Leave entitlements, generous holidays & leave conditions

Opportunities for advancement and mobility across the Department to support your career growth 







How to apply 

Attach a resume (maximum 5 pages) and a cover letter (maximum 2 pages) outlining how you meet the requirements and capabilities of this role. 




Targeted Question 


1. Outline your experience in the design, delivery and maintenance of business intelligence strategies and governance protocols. (300 words) 




2. Describe your approach engaging with a diverse range of stakeholders to meet their needs and achieve the desired business outcomes. (300 words) 







Applicants are encouraged to prepare their application materials independently. The selection panel values authentic and personally written responses that reflect your own experience, insights and alignment with the Department’s values and priorities. 




Please refer to the Applicant’s Guide to Applying to assist you with your application. 




If you are an existing Education Support Staff (corporate staff) with the NSW Department of Education, click here for instructions on how to submit your application. 




All other applicants please submit your application by clicking ‘Apply Now’. 

 
Note: the selection process will include a range of assessment techniques to assist in determining your suitability for the role. Successful candidates will be required to undertake pre-employment screening for this role which includes a National Criminal History Check. 




As a condition of employment, successful candidates not currently employed by the Department must provide a valid Working with Children Check (WWCC) clearance number. Renewal is not required after the WWCC expires, as long as you remain in a non–child-related role. Employer obligations only apply while the WWCC is valid. 




Candidates must have Australian work rights (citizens, permanent residents, New Zealand citizens or a valid work visa). Ongoing (permanent) roles are open to Australian citizens and permanent residents and New Zealand citizens, while temporary roles welcome applicants with a visa.  

 
Closing date:  Thursday, 28 May 2026 at 11:59pm.  

 
For role enquiries please contact Jessica Horn via email on Jessica.Horn6@det.nsw.edu.au and for recruitment process enquiries please contact projectrecruitment@det.nsw.edu.au  

 
We welcome applications from all ages and genders, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, culturally and linguistically diverse groups, the LGBTQIA+ community, veterans, refugees and people with disability. If we can make some adjustments to our recruitment/interview process to better enable you to shine, please contact the Diversity and Inclusion Team (youbelong@det.nsw.edu.au) or visit NSW Department of Education Diversity and Inclusion.

Ad Text:
Full time, Ongoing position
Salary range: $131,154 - $155,000 plus superannuation
Up to $15,900 in salary packaging, flexible hybrid work arrangement

About the role

The Data Integrity Lead ensures Victoria Legal Aid (VLA)'s data is consistently defined and documented, so it can be trusted, its semantic and meaning understood, and used effectively across the organisation and with external partners. The role leads the development and governance of VLA's metadata, including data dictionaries and business glossaries, and coordinates the governance of priority data domains. 

As operational owner of VLA's enterprise metadata management platform, the role works with digital project teams to embed agreed definitions and standards into new and enhanced systems, and with the Data Reporting and Advanced Analytics and AI teams to ensure data products use agreed data elements, metrics and calculations. The Data Integrity Lead collaborates closely with the Enterprise Data Architect and Data Quality Lead to align approaches, identify priority data domains, and represents VLA in selected external forums to support alignment with sector-wide definitions and standards.

This role is based at our Melbourne CBD office on Wurundjeri Country. 

About you

As our ideal candidate, you will possess:

Proven ability to lead metadata and data definition work in a complex public service or regulated organisation, producing accurate, accessible, and trusted business metadata and related governance artefacts.
Knowledge and practical experience in master data, reference, transactional data and organisational metrics governance, including identifying authoritative sources, business rules, and aligning practices with external standards where relevant.
Experience managing enterprise metadata management systems, including administration, configuration, and content quality oversight.
Highly developed analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to assess data-related risks, identify gaps or inconsistencies, and recommend practical solutions and controls.
Strong collaboration and relationship management skills, with a track record of working across functions, including system and data design, analytics, architecture, and data quality teams, to achieve shared outcomes.
Excellent communication skills, with the ability to translate complex data governance concepts into clear, plain language for technical and non-technical audiences.
A knowledge of the general business of VLA and a commitment to our vision and values.

Reporting to the Manager, Cyber Risk & Compliance, this is a data and analytics-focused role, where you will design and deliver high-quality dashboards, metrics, data models, and insights that support decision-making at senior leadership and Board levels.

Job Title: Senior Valuations Analyst - Heavy Equipment Auction Industry
Ad Text:
Senior Valuation Analyst role

Move, Build, Grow your career with Ritchie Bros.

Ritchie Bros. is a global leader in asset management and equipment sales. We help thousands of people around the world buy and sell heavy equipment every month, trusted for our innovation, customer service, and global reach.

The Senior Valuation Analyst is a senior individual contributor role within Commercial Operations responsible for assigning contract value through accurate, consistent, and market-informed asset pricing. This role’s sole focus is valuation: pricing assets in support of commercial deals and ensuring pricing outputs align with established frameworks, market dynamics, and financial objectives.

The Senior Valuation Analyst operates as an experienced pricing specialist, applying judgment to complex or high impact valuation scenarios. This role does not own deal structure, approvals, or commercial negotiations; instead, it partners closely with Deal Management and Commercial Program Management by providing pricing inputs that enable informed commercial decision-making.

The Valuation function includes Valuation Analysts and Senior Valuation Analysts, differentiated by experience, complexity handled, and level of judgment applied—not by scope outside of pricing.

This role could be located in Yatala, Brisbane or Geelong, Victoria - With Hybrid work set up.

What you’ll do

Assign contract value by pricing assets accurately and consistently across standard and non-standard deals.

• Apply approved valuation methodologies, pricing frameworks, and market data to determine asset value.

• Ensure pricing outputs are complete, defensible, and aligned with commercial and financial objectives.

Partner with Deal Management to support end-to-end deal execution through timely and accurate pricing inputs.

Handle more complex, high-value, or non-standard valuation scenarios requiring advanced judgment.

Stay informed on market trends, asset values, and relevant data sources impacting valuation.

Identify when pricing scenarios fall outside standard parameters and flag considerations to Deal Management or Commercial Programs.

What you’ll bring

5–8+ years of experience in valuation, pricing, financial analysis, commercial operations, or a related field.

Strong analytical skills and experience assigning asset or contract value.

Demonstrated ability to apply judgment within established pricing frameworks.

Experience working in a fast-paced, cross-functional commercial environment.

Strong attention to detail and commitment to pricing accuracy and consistency.

Clear communication skills, particularly when explaining pricing assumptions and outputs.

What we offer

Competitive base salary plus annual bonus

Comprehensive health and dental benefits for the whole family

Income Protection, TPD and Life Insurance

Employee Share Program and EAP

Gym membership & Tuition reimbursement*

Ongoing training, leadership development, and career growth opportunities.

A stable, international business with a supportive local team.

About the role

Our client, a Sydney-based critical infrastructure business, is hiring a Data Platform & Governance Lead to own delivery of two foundational programmes running in parallel: a Microsoft Purview implementation that establishes the organisation's information governance, PII, and DLP posture, and a Databricks-based enterprise data platform with Power BI reporting on top.

This is an outcome-ownership role, not a pure delivery-management appointment or a hands-on engineering-build role. You will own the outcome of both programmes, hold suppliers accountable for technical delivery, and drop in personally where your judgement and design instinct make the difference.
 
What will you own

End-to-end outcome of both programmes (Purview and Databricks) to budget, scope, and timeline.
The Microsoft Purview rollout, working with an external IT delivery partner and Legal to shape classification regimes, sensitivity labels, DLP rules, and the post-implementation roadmap of flags and compliance reporting.
Scoping, chartering, and shaping the Databricks platform programme in parallel with Purview, engaging the right external delivery partner, and subsequently owning delivery to plan.
Design of a Power BI reporting suite over Databricks, with a fresh look at business progress reporting as the foundation use case.
Translating technical work into the risk, capability, and value language an executive audience needs.
 
What you will bring

Demonstrated outcome ownership of data platform or information governance programmes in complex, multi-stakeholder environments.
Hands-on experience with Microsoft Purview, or a closely comparable information governance, DLP, or sensitivity labelling platform. You will need to design classification regimes, not just deploy the tool.
Working knowledge of modern cloud data platforms, ideally Databricks. Snowflake, Microsoft Fabric, or equivalent acceptable.
Proven supplier and stakeholder management across complex multi-party delivery (internal teams, IT partners, external implementation partners, Legal).
Strong workshop facilitation, particularly with Legal and risk functions on classification and compliance design.
Strong executive communication, comfortable translating technical detail into business risk and capability language.
Comfort with AI-augmented ways of working, using modern AI tooling (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, or similar) as a standard part of how you scope, document, and oversee delivery.
High agency, commercial pragmatism, and the judgement to know when to lead from the front and when to delegate.
 
Desirable

Experience with Power BI or comparable BI tooling, particularly designing reporting suites over a modern data platform.
Microsoft ecosystem certifications (Purview, Azure, Power Platform) or Databricks certifications.
Background that bridges consulting and in-house delivery.
Exposure to Cyber and Information Security work, particularly in the context of AI risks and capabilities.
Sector experience in telecommunications, infrastructure, asset management, digital infrastructure, or commercial property.
 
What this role is not

A pure programme management or PMO position.
A security-only appointment without data platform or governance ownership. 
How to apply

Please include a CV and a short note on your most relevant Purview and data platform delivery experience.

Ad Text:
The Reserve Bank's Enterprise Portfolio Management Office (EPMO) is a centralised function that is responsible for providing effective governance over the Bank's strategic investment portfolio.


Our objectives are to strengthen project management practices, provide assurance and oversight across the Bank's portfolio and guide improved project governance and delivery. We support the evaluation, prioritisation, and ongoing monitoring of the Bank's project portfolio. We also provide advice and support to the Investment Committee.


In this position you will provide project control and assurance support ensuring consistent application of the policies, principles, practices and Ways of Working for change initiatives across the project portfolio.

Provide assurance against projects and ensure they are adhering to the Governance and Delivery standards and progressing in alignment with the direction agreed with EPMO & other support partners

Assist with the PM Community of Practice as required.
Other duties as required.

Demonstrated experience in project assurance across Waterfall and Agile projects.
Proven experience within EPMO, PMO functions, or project delivery roles.
Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to build trust and rapport at all levels - particularly with Project Managers.
Solid understanding of the end-to-end project delivery lifecycle.

Why RBA?
The RBA makes an important contribution to the Australian economy through the pursuit of national economic policy objectives and associated activities in financial markets and banking. We also issue Australia's banknotes and operate infrastructure critical to the payments system, all of which contribute to the welfare of the Australian people.


The Reserve Bank of Australia is committed to equity, diversity and inclusion through key initiatives. We welcome and encourage applicants from diverse backgrounds to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, culturally and linguistically diverse background, those living with a disability and from the LGBTQ+ community. About the role

Our client are at a pivotal moment with the consolidation and transformation of their data infrastructure into a unified enterprise architecture & need a senior data engineer with expert level Snowflake who can drive us in the right direction.

You will be the most senior and experienced data engineer on the team, setting standards, defining best practices, and playing a central role in shaping how we build and operate data systems going forward. You'll work closely with our data architect to plan and execute a migration from our current state to a modern, scalable future.

It's hands-on, strategic, and high-impact with the view to promote this hire into a Lead Data Engineer within 12 months.

*NB: YOU MUST BE LOCATED IN SYDNEY AND HAVE AUSTRALIAN PERMANENT RESIDENCY OR CITZENSHIP**

The responsibilities

Lead the planning and execution of our cloud data warehouse consolidation (Redshift to Snowflake), working hand-in-hand with the data architect and wider team
Define and enforce engineering standards, coding best practices, and ways of working across the data engineering team
Maintain and improve existing Airflow ETL pipelines while technical debt is addressed and future-state systems are built out
Design and build robust, scalable data pipelines ingesting from REST APIs, Graph APIs, S3, SharePoint, and a variety of file formats
Administer Snowflake environments, user access, data sharing, roles, warehouses, and schemas across global teams
Provide technical guidance and oversight to onshore & offshore data engineering resources
Collaborate with BI, DevOps, IT, and occasionally senior leadership to translate technical decisions for non-technical stakeholders

The requirements

Essential:

Strong Snowflake expertise - including admin, data sharing, role-based access, multi-warehouse environments
Strong AWS experience (Redshift/S3) accompanied with Python and SQL expertise
Deep understanding of ETL/ELT design and pipeline architecture
Airflow experience (or equivalent orchestration tool - Prefect, Dagster, etc.)
Proven ability to consume REST and Graph APIs, including authentication (OAuth, tokens, refresh flows) and rate limiting
Strong data system design skills & ideally coming from a software engineering background (version control, code review, CI/CD mindset)
Comfortable operating autonomously and setting standards in a small, high-trust team

Desirable:

Cloud-to-cloud data warehouse migration experience
Kubernetes familiarity
Snowflake certification or Snowflake Cortex or AI/ML tooling exposure

The interview process

Culture & behavioural interview: Situational questions, ways of working, and mutual fit
Technical take-home + live review: A practical task followed by a deeper technical conversation covering system design thinking and broader experience
In-office presentation: Meet the wider team and present to a mixed technical and non-technical audience

Job Title: Senior Business Analyst - Process Redesign - Dynamics 365 ERP
Ad Text:
Be part of an exciting business systems modernisation program.
Work for a Private Company which has looked after its employees for over 50 years.
Great working environment and rewarding salary on offer

This is a great opportunity for you to join an established, successful and respected industry leader. 

About Us...

Australian owned and operated, Alspec is the market leader in the design and distribution of innovative aluminium systems to the architectural, residential, industrial and home-improvement markets.

Our continuous investment in research and development ensures the latest technologies and design principles are applied in our extensive range of aluminium systems and products. We are committed to providing world-class service and building successful partnerships with all our customers.

Alspec has 15 locations throughout Australia, with over 750 employees. 

About the Role...

Reporting to the Chief Technology Officer, the The Senior Business Process Analyst will lead the redefinition of business operational processes across the organisation.

They will leverage Lean Six Sigma methodologies to redefine how the business operates, and how employees interact with a Dynamics 365 ERP.

Key responsibilities:

The Business Process Analyst is to implement a business process management framework to systematically document processes, including process mapping, defining key performance indicators and controls, and identifying risks.
The primary focus for the Business Analyst is to document the as-is current flow of process, then redefine new Lean processes to achieve the desired outcome in the most efficient way possible.
The outputs from this Process Analyst will flow into an active D365 ERP modernisation program of work.
Working closely with business stakeholders, you will carry out 'As-Is' analysis, identify how to optimise the process, and design a refined process to uplift efficiency. You will design the future target state, as well as the optimisation and uplift of productivity. Your Lean processes will be input to the modernisation of our ERP, the design of the D365 UI, will reduce time and effort, and redefine the most efficient path for the business.

About You...

Experience:

Active and current Lean Six Sigma certification (Minimum Green Belt, Black Belt preferred).
Proven experience defining business processes for Dynamics 365 or CRM/ERP transformation programs.
Exceptional strength in documenting processes, and measured improvements to achieve business outcomes.
Deep experience engaging various business users, across large multi‑disciplinary business functions.
Strong analytical skills with a focus on detail, efficiency, and automated flows.
Ability to balance pace with quality in complex enterprise environments.
Exceptional communication, prioritisation, people engagement, and decision-making skills.

In addition, you will be;

Pragmatic and outcome-focused, and comfortable influencing senior stakeholder and technical teams.
Very organised, strategic thinker, meticulously thorough individual, with strong problem-solving skills.

   

You may be required to undergo a pre-employment medical including drug and alcohol testing and must have contactable work references.

Do Work That Matters 

Leidos Australia delivers IT and airborne solutions that protect and advance the Australian way of life.  Our 2000 local experts, backed by our global experience and network of partners, are working to solve the world’s toughest challenges in government, intelligence, defence, aviation, border protection and health markets.  




Your New Role and Responsibilities  

The Reporting & Governance Lead is responsible for establishing, maintaining, and continuously improving the reporting ecosystem and governance framework that ensures transparency, compliance, auditability, and performance accountability across the sustainment program.

The role provides leadership across performance reporting, data integrity, governance forums, ASDEFCON‑mandated reporting, decision‑tracking, backlog governance, and Secretariat functions for program oversight bodies.

Own and operate the day-to-day governance framework, ensuring alignment with ASDEFCON controls, contractual requirements and mandated governance pathways

Establish, manage and coordinate structured governance forums and calendars, including agendas, minutes, actions, decisions and escalation processes

Lead the design and delivery of program reporting across all domains, owning CDRL-aligned deliverables and ensuring accuracy, timeliness and auditability

Provide governance insight through performance reporting, trend analysis, risk identification and executive-level commentary

Maintain governance oversight of compliance, documentation quality, backlogs, gaps, exemptions and contract change recommendations across the program lifecycle

Build strong relationships and coordinate across engineering, security, sustainment and subcontractor teams to ensure consistent, contract-aligned governance execution




What You’ll Bring to Make An Impact  

Proven governance, reporting or PMO leadership experience within complex ICT programs or service environments

Strong analytical capability, with the ability to interpret multi-source performance data and translate insights into executive-level reporting

Demonstrated experience coordinating senior governance forums, including preparation of agendas, papers, minutes, registers and decision logs

Working knowledge of regulated compliance environments (e.g. ASDEFCON, ISM, risk and performance management frameworks)

Strong stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to lead cross-functional teams across multi-vendor and multidisciplinary environments

Highly organised, resilient and detail-focused, with a commitment to transparency, accuracy and contractual compliance

Excellent clinical, time management, organisational and communication skills, with the ability to adapt and problem solve.

Ad Text:
At McNab, we recruit people who want more than just the average job. The people who thrive here are the ones who enjoy solving complex problems, improving the way businesses operate, and taking ownership of meaningful outcomes.

We’re building a modern data capability with strong backing from executive leadership, and we’re looking for a Senior Data Engineer who wants the freedom to help shape that journey.

About the Role

As a Senior Data Engineer at McNab, you’ll play a key role in designing and evolving our modern data platform across Azure, Snowflake and Airflow.

This role is more than building pipelines — we’re looking for someone who can think strategically, identify opportunities for improvement, influence technical direction, and proactively solve problems across the broader business.

You’ll work closely with the Data & Analytics Manager and wider teams to design scalable data solutions, improve engineering standards, and help drive innovation across our growing technology ecosystem. We’re particularly interested in people who enjoy experimenting with new tools, leveraging AI and automation, and continuously improving the way teams work.

This is an opportunity to join a business where technology investment is strongly supported from the top down, and where good ideas can quickly turn into action.

Key responsibilities include:

Assist in shaping and delivering McNab’s data engineering roadmap.
Design, build and maintain scalable data ingestion pipelines using Airflow.
Develop and optimise enterprise-grade data models and warehouse solutions.
Continuously improve pipeline performance, scalability, reliability and observability.
Build and maintain CI/CD processes using GitHub Actions.
Leverage LLMs and automation tooling to improve engineering workflows and code quality.
Support data governance, security and access management requirements across the business.
Collaborate closely with analytics, applications and business teams to deliver fit-for-purpose solutions.
Contribute to engineering standards, documentation and best practices across the team.

About You

You’re someone who enjoys ownership, thinks ahead, and doesn’t wait to be told where improvements can be made.

You’ll likely bring:

Demonstrated experience as a Data Engineer or in a similar role.
Strong experience building modern cloud-based data platforms.
The ability to work autonomously and confidently lead technical outcomes.
Strong communication skills and the ability to collaborate across technical and non-technical teams.
A proactive mindset with a genuine interest in solving business problems, not just technical tasks.
A passion for continuous improvement, innovation and emerging technologies.
Experience mentoring or positively influencing other team members through collaboration and leadership.

Technical Skills:

Advanced Python skills for data engineering and processing complex data structures.
Expert-level SQL skills including optimisation, modelling and security.
Strong experience with Snowflake, Airflow and modern cloud infrastructure.
Experience with Azure resources including Virtual Machines, Function Apps and Logic Apps.
Strong experience with Git, GitHub Actions and CI/CD principles.
Comfortable working within Linux/terminal-based environments.
Interest and practical exposure to AI-assisted development workflows and LLM tooling.

Desired Skills & Experience:

Experience working alongside Data Science, DevOps or Software Engineering teams.
Experience designing enterprise-scale data models and modern data architectures.
Exposure to analytics engineering or data application development environments.

About McNab

McNab is one of the largest Construction, Development and Property groups in Australia, with turnover now exceeding $1bn each year. Despite our growth and size, we've never lost our small company DNA - which centres around genuine care for our staff, customers and all our stakeholders. Success for us isn't about awards or growth, it's when we deliver so well that our customers are proud to wear a McNab T-Shirt on weekends!

We genuinely care. We make it easy. We deliver certainty.

This is our promise - to our clients, and everyone we deal with. Delivering on these three things really well is what drives us at McNab. Everything we do, from recruiting and training to performance and promotion, centres around this promise.

We leverage the entire McNab Group to deliver certainty for our clients at a time of unprecedented uncertainty in the industry. And our approach works. From delivering our own property developments, to manufacturing our own building products, and a plethora of aligned businesses, your career at McNab goes well beyond traditional construction roles.

McNab benefits:

Birthday leave – Celebrate your special day on us!
Bank and Employee Discounts - Access a range of exclusive savings and offers.
Social Club and team events – Work hard, play hard with regular team events.
Annual health and fitness subsidy – Stay fit and healthy with our health benefits
Fitness passport membership - Access to a wide range of gyms and fitness facilities
Parental leave program – Supporting you through life’s important milestones with tailored options to you and your family
Employee Assistance Program – We’ve got your back when you need it
Tailored development opportunities – We invest in your growth

This is an outstanding opportunity to join a business genuinely investing in technology and data, where you’ll have the opportunity to influence direction, implement new ideas and help shape the future capability of the organisation.

If this sounds like the kind of opportunity you’ve been looking for — APPLY NOW!

At Aurecon, we're an Australian-owned design, engineering and advisory company with a proud history of helping clients manage their assets across the full lifecycle. With strong growth across Australia, we're seeking Consultants to join our national Asset Management Advisory team.

This is an opportunity to move beyond traditional consulting and apply your skills to projects that improve the performance, reliability and sustainability of the infrastructure and assets communities rely on every day.

How you'll make an impact

You'll work with clients across both the public and private sectors, contributing to projects that drive operational excellence and sustainable outcomes. From day one, you'll build your consulting toolkit while gaining hands-on experience in engineering‑led initiatives.

Your work will include:

Supporting end‑to‑end project delivery, from analysis through to client engagement and measurable outcomes.
Translating technical data and insights into clear, actionable advice for clients.
Contributing to solutions in areas such as asset management strategies, Asset decision-making, performance improvement, system design, and asset condition assessments.
Collaborating with senior advisors on initiatives spanning operational readiness, reliability engineering, productivity improvement, capital portfolio management and complex, multi-disciplinary projects..

What you'll bring

As a Data Engineer, you'll join a rapidly growing team within an emerging part of the business. Your technical capability and curiosity will help you work confidently with clients and internal stakeholders across diverse environments.

We're looking for someone with:

Experience across the software development life cycle, including containerisation, Git and data science languages/notebooks
Strong data engineering skills: cleansing, transformation, loading and modelling across SQL and NoSQL
Familiarity with major cloud platforms (Azure, AWS or GCP) and their storage, database, data lake and analytics services
Experience with key data and ML tools such as Spark, Databricks, Python, R, TensorFlow/PyTorch and Jupyter/RStudio
Understanding of data visualisation (e.g., Power BI), agile ways of working and holding a relevant scientific or engineering degree

Ad Text:
Who we are:

Milwaukee Tools is an industry leader in the manufacturing of cordless & electric power tools, accessories, and hand tools. We pride ourselves on an unrelenting strategic focus on powerful brands, innovative products and exceptional people. Our culture is centred around improving the lives of our users, something that not only drives our product development but underpins everything we do.

We are committed to ensuring our people have a great experience at Milwaukee, so in addition to an exciting and challenging role we also provide a range of great employee benefits including:

Lifestyle: Hybrid working model.
Discounts & Perks: Generous discounts on company products, and novated leasing available.
Personal Development: Access to professional development programs.
Health & Wellbeing: Paid Parental Leave and Employee Assistance Program.
Social: Recognition programs and social events to celebrate our team's achievements.

Reporting to the Customer Experience Manager, the Voice of the Customer Manager plays a pivotal role in strengthening how Milwaukee listens, learns and improves. You'll partner closely with the Customer Experience Manager, Head of Customer Experience & Insights and the wider CX & Insights team to build a deep understanding of our users and partners - their desires, frustrations and buying behaviours - and use that insight to shape how Milwaukee competes and improves the end-to-end experience.

This is a high-impact role where you'll own and drive Milwaukee's Voice of Customer program and work cross-functionally to turn insight into action and drive tangible improvements across the business.

What you will bring to the role:

5+ years in Voice of Customer, Customer Experience or Research & Insights (including market research agencies).
Familiarity with Voice of Customer Platforms (InMoment, Press Ganey Forsta, Qualtrics or Medallia).
Broad experience in quantitative research methodologies, including statistical analysis techniques such as driver analysis and correspondence mapping.
Familiarity with Q Research Software to run tables and conduct analysis.
Experience using AI tools to analyse and synthesise customer feedback and insights.
Ability to integrate AI into workflows to improve efficiency and insight generation, with an understanding of responsible AI use and data considerations.

What your day-today look like:

Lead the Voice of Customer program

Translate customer feedback into clear, actionable insights through regular reporting and dashboards.
Partner with stakeholders to embed Voice of Customer insight into decision making.
Drive continuous improvement initiatives based on customer feedback and performance metrics.

Optimise the Voice of Customer platform

Lead ongoing development and rebuild of our VoC platform, ensuring a seamless transition through stakeholder & supplier engagement and integration assistance.
Ensure strong data quality, survey design integrity and reporting accuracy.

Connect insight to performance

Track and report on the impact of VoC initiatives on customer and business outcomes.
Guide the business in setting meaningful customer KPIs and targets.
Link customer metrics to commercial outcomes such as retention, growth and performance.

Influence and enable the business

Present clear, compelling insights and recommendations to stakeholders.
Act as a trusted advisor across Sales, Marketing, Operations and JSS to drive customer-led decision making.

If you're ready to own the voice of the customer and drive real change - then please click APPLY NOW!

Role highlights: Strategic Commercial Finance Manager role in a fast growth business



Role Overview

Reporting to the CFO with close partnership with Channel leads, Head of Manufacturing & Chief Commercial Officer, this role is the commercial finance heartbeat of the business. You'll establish and build the FP&A / Commercial function, establish commercial gatekeeping that didn't previously exist, and become the trusted advisor ensuring every growth opportunity is profitable.

As the organisation onboards major retailers, you'll present P&Ls to retail partners, lead range reviews, assess NPD launches, and ensure commercial decisions create win-win partnerships. You'll own the S&OP process globally, working with channel leads and inventory teams to ensure stock levels support growth without tying up working capital.

Beyond the commercial partnering, you'll transform data chaos into clarity. An incredibly high bar has been set through the creation of company-wide Power BI dashboards that everyone now depends on. You'll continue this legacy, identifying system gaps, optimising where you can, and deliver analytics that drive decision-making at the executive level.

This is a hands-on, standalone role for the first 12 months with weekly touchpoints to the CFO. You'll be in the weeds while thinking strategically. As the business matures, you'll build your own team with potential for significant expansion



Responsibilities


Build FP&A Function from Scratch

Deliver annual budgets with bottom-up three-way models (B/S, Cashflow, P&L). Prepare presentations for the Senior Leadership Team. Undertake regular reforecasting and build budget plans with market heads. Introduce ROI metrics around marketing and strategic investments.


Commercial Gatekeeping and Business Partnering

Partner with Chief Commercial Officer and channel leads to assess commercial opportunities and filter unprofitable growth. Lead range reviews with Woolworths, Walmart, and other retail partners. Conduct NPD commercial business case reviews and assist Chief Growth Officer in gatekeeping new product launches.


Own S&OP Process Globally

Represent finance in demand forecast sign-offs globally. Run meetings with channel leads and head of inventory. Introduce initiatives to reduce inventory exposure and optimize working capital.


Advanced Analytics and Insights

Provide accurate analysis and reporting of Sales and COGS in monthly CFO packs for each market and segment. Conduct product-level profitability and volume-mix analysis. Work with CFO to assess and implement the right BI tools.


Systems Transformation

Systemise Actuals vs Forecast vs Budget reporting. Identify and fix data structure gaps.


Strategic Finance Support

Assist CFO in managing FX risk exposures and develop hedging strategy. Analyse and optimise banking platforms and fees. Develop long-term financial valuation models. Partner on sales and commission plan design.



Candidate Profile

You're a progressive finance professional who's built your career in FMCG or product-led businesses. You've been there, done that with retail partnerships and S&OP, and you thrive in fast-paced, high-growth environments where no two days are the same.

You're not just technically brilliant - you're commercially savvy. You understand how to turn data into stories that influence decision-making. You can present to CEOs and retail partners with confidence, simplifying complexity and driving action.

Power BI is your playground. You've built dashboards from scratch, mined data from imperfect sources, and created analytics infrastructure that becomes mission-critical. Advanced Excel and financial modelling are second nature.

Most importantly, you're entrepreneurial and self-directed. You don't need to be told what to do - you identify gaps, create solutions, and drive outcomes. You're comfortable with ambiguity and bring structure without perfectionism. You value speed over bureaucracy and results over politics.

This isn't a role for those who need hierarchy, process, or hand-holding. It's for the person who wants to build something meaningful, work alongside an entrepreneurial CEO, and scale a global business.



Company, Culture & Benefits

Our client is a global leader in their field, helping millions of people worldwide reach their goals. Founded 20 years ago, they've built one of the most recognised brands in their field, with their flagship products becoming the #1 product in its category globally.

The culture is fast-paced, values-driven, and collaborative. There's no red tape, no corporate politics, and no unnecessary hierarchy. You'll work directly with the CEO, present to the executive team, and have genuine influence over strategy. People are direct, authentic, and passionate about building something meaningful together.

If you thrive in scale-up environments, love creating structure from ambiguity, and want to build the FP&A and commercial finance function for a globally recognized brand, this is your opportunity.



Scale with the business - expand your remit and build your team as the company grows
Be rewarded - $170-180K + super + 20% STI + future ESOP + free gym membership + product discounts
Make genuine impact - be pivotal in shaping the business as it scales toward an equity event



Apply

If this sounds like you, please send your resume by clicking the "apply" link below. The reference number for this role is LFCFM65336. Please allow for 3 - 5 working days for us to process all the applications. All applicants will receive feedback from us, either via email or a telephone call.
